title: clematopsis scabiosifolia subsp. oliveri
intro: Clematopsis scabiosifolia subsp. oliveri, also known as Oliver's clematopsis, is a plant species that belongs to the family Caprifoliaceae and is native to New South Wales in Australia. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as colds and bronchitis, due to its expectorant properties. The health benefits of Clematopsis scabiosifolia subsp. oliveri include its ability to relieve coughs, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable plant in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of this plant often involve infusions or decoctions, and it can be used in the form of tea, tinctures, or poultices, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
